questions.text: What is the special virtue of reciting Surah Al-Kahf?
questions.answer: It provides protection from the trial of the Dajjal (Antichrist) and provides light until the next Friday.

questions.text: What are the four main stories mentioned in Surah Al-Kahf?
questions.answer: The People of the Cave, the Owner of the Two Gardens, Musa and Khidr, and Dhul-Qarnayn.

questions.text: How many young men were in the cave and how long did they sleep?
questions.answer: Their exact number is known only to Allah (though 3, 5, or 7 are discussed), and they stayed for 309 lunar years.

questions.text: Why did the young men flee to the cave?
questions.answer: To protect their faith (Iman) from a tyrannical, pagan king.

questions.text: What phrase must a believer say when planning for the future (Verse 23-24)?
questions.answer: In Shaa Allah (If Allah wills).

questions.text: In the story of the Two Gardens, what was the sin of the owner of the better garden?
questions.answer: Arrogance, ungratefulness to Allah, and doubting the arrival of the Last Hour.

questions.text: What happened to the gardens of the arrogant man?
questions.answer: They were destroyed by a storm from the sky, leaving them a barren waste.

questions.text: To what is the life of this world compared in Verse 45?
questions.answer: To vegetation that flourishes after rain but then dries up and is scattered by the wind.

questions.text: What are 'the adornments of the life of this world'?
questions.answer: Wealth and children.

questions.text: Why did Musa (AS) go on a journey to find 'one of Our servants' (Khidr)?
questions.answer: To seek higher knowledge and wisdom that he did not possess.

questions.text: What were the three actions Khidr took that confused Musa (AS)?
questions.answer: Damaging a ship, killing a boy, and repairing a wall in a town that refused them hospitality.

questions.text: Why did Khidr damage the ship?
questions.answer: To make it defective so a tyrannical king, who was seizing every good ship by force, would leave it for the poor owners.

questions.text: Why did Khidr kill the boy?
questions.answer: The boy was destined to be a disbeliever and would have oppressed his righteous parents; Allah intended to replace him with a better child.

questions.text: Why did Khidr repair the wall?
questions.answer: It belonged to two orphan boys and hidden beneath it was a treasure for them; Allah wanted it preserved until they reached maturity.

questions.text: Who was Dhul-Qarnayn?
questions.answer: A powerful, righteous king and traveler who was given means and authority to establish justice on earth.

questions.text: What did Dhul-Qarnayn build to protect a people from Gog and Magog (Ya'juj and Ma'juj)?
questions.answer: A massive barrier/wall made of iron blocks and molten copper.

questions.text: When will the barrier of Gog and Magog break?
questions.answer: When the promise of Allah (the End Times) comes, Allah will level it to the ground.

questions.text: Who are the 'greatest losers' in terms of their deeds (Verse 103)?
questions.answer: Those whose effort is lost in worldly life while they think they are doing well.

questions.text: What would happen if the sea were ink for the words of Allah?
questions.answer: The sea would be exhausted before the words of my Lord were finished, even if another sea were brought to supplement it.

questions.text: What is the final message of the Surah in Verse 110?
questions.answer: The Prophet is but a man like others, but receives revelation that your God is One God; so whoever hopes for the meeting with his Lord should do righteous deeds and not associate anyone in worship.
